title: WebSiteMCP Binding
description: Meta-schema for a WebSiteMCP binding: a recording that implements a contract's methods for one site. A binding is authored once by recording in-page input, output, and actions; it is the concrete, private, site-specific implementation of the contract. Lives at domain/<reverse-DNS path>/<contract>@<version>.json, beside the site's manifest. Part of WebSiteMCP spec version 1.0.

contract.description: Contract category this binding implements. Must match the filename stem before '@'.

satisfiesContract.description: The exact contract version this recording implements. Must equal the manifest's pinned contract version.

version.description: Binding version. Bumps when the recording changes (redesign, relocator, flow fix). Free to move under a fixed satisfiesContract. Must match the '@<version>' in the filename.

methods.description: One recording per implemented method, keyed by method name. Keys must equal the manifest's declared methods for this contract.

recording.description: The captured step sequence for one method, plus the error map applied during execution.

errors.description: Maps recognized failure conditions to contract error codes. A raised code MUST be one of the codes declared for this method in the contract.

step.description: One recorded action. The 'action' discriminator selects which fields apply.

note.description: Optional human label captured at record time.

url.description: navigate: absolute or relative URL. May contain {{param}} tokens.

target.description: Ordered, multi-strategy locator for the element. Required for click/input/select/submit/read and for waitFor/assert when condition is selector-based.

value.description: input/select: the value to enter. Use {{paramName}} to inject a contract param (the only valid bind mechanism for inputs). Literal text is allowed for fixed values.

wait.description: waitFor: the synchronization condition. Also allowed on any step as a pre-condition to satisfy before the step runs.

assert.description: assert: condition that must hold, else the step fails (and may map to an error).

extract.description: read: pull a value from the page into a named return field (the output side of the bind map).

locators.description: Candidate locators in priority order. Replay tries each in turn until one resolves to a unique element. Order from most resilient (role/testid) to most brittle (css/xpath).

strategy.description: role=ARIA role; testid=stable test attribute; label=associated form label; text=visible text; css/xpath=raw selectors.

value.description: Selector or value for the chosen strategy (e.g. role -> 'button', css -> '#amount').

name.description: Accessible name / additional qualifier (e.g. role 'button' name 'Transfer money').

type.description: selector=element present; selectorGone=element removed; url=location matches; networkIdle=no in-flight requests; timeout=fixed delay.

url.description: URL or pattern for type 'url'. May contain {{param}} tokens.

timeoutMs.description: Max wait (or fixed delay for type 'timeout'). Exceeding it is a failure.

field.description: Return-schema property this value populates. MUST be a property of the method's contract 'returns'.

from.description: Where to read: visible text, form value, or an attribute.

as.description: Coerce the captured string to this return type.

when.description: The condition that signals this error. Exactly one form per rule.

raise.description: Contract error code to raise. Validated against the contract method's declared errors at publish time.
